title: Learning Record
description: A first-class, reviewable record of a proposed change derived from reviewer feedback (release-spec.md §7.10; model §2; DD-6). Learning is never in-place mutation: a record captures the signals it was derived from, the artifact it targets, that artifact's mutability class (recorded before apply), the proposed diff, and a status. In v1 records are created (proposed) and applied by a human only; application snapshots the instance repo and a rollback ref enables one-step revert. Records that target human-only artifacts (guardrail/safety rules) are refused by the application tool. All refs are $CONTENT_HOME-relative or instance-repo commit refs; absolute paths are forbidden. Structural contract only; instances are instance data and never ship in the repo.

id.description: Stable identifier for the learning record.

source_signals.description: The reviewer/analytics signals the proposal was derived from. Minimum-signal thresholds apply (DD-6); a single-signal (n=1) proposal is flagged.

refs.description: $CONTENT_HOME-relative references to the source artifacts (decisions, reports, calibration runs).

count.description: Number of occurrences supporting the signal; count=1 is flagged per DD-6.

target_artifact.description: Identifier of the artifact the change targets (e.g. a rule id, brand DNA file, or config key).

target_mutability.description: Mutability class of the target, recorded before apply (model §3 rule 4). human-only targets are refused by the application tool.

proposed_diff.description: The proposed change as a diff against the target artifact.

applied_by.description: Identifier of the human who applied the change (human-only in v1; machine application is roadmap). Present once status is applied.

applied_at.description: When the change was applied. Present once status is applied.

rollback_ref.description: Instance-repo commit ref captured before application, enabling one-step rollback (DD-6). Present once status is applied.

shareability.description: Whether the record's diff is a candidate for a sanitized opt-in upstream PR (DD-7(b)); no automated transmission exists in v1.
